notes: Entity confirmed: Wikipedia 'Modern Jazz Quartet (MJQ)... established in 1952', disbanded 1997. birthYear 1952 and deathYear 1997 match ('disbanded permanently in 1997'). Instruments [piano, vibraphone, drums, bass] match the quartet's instrumentation (John Lewis piano, Milt Jackson vibraphone, drummers, Percy Heath double bass). Bio is editorial framing (Cool Jazz).

Entity confirmed: Wikipedia 'Johnny Dodds (April 12, 1892 – August 8, 1940) was an American jazz clarinetist and alto saxophonist'. birthYear 1892, deathYear 1940, instruments [alto saxophone, clarinet] all match. Bio is editorial (Early Jazz).

Entity confirmed: Wikipedia 'Theodore "Fats" Navarro (September 24, 1923 – July 7, 1950)... American jazz trumpet player'. birthYear 1923, deathYear 1950, instruments [trumpet] all match. (Wikipedia text contains an internal July 6 vs July 7 inconsistency for the day of death, but the year 1950 is firm.) Bio editorial (Bebop).

Entity confirmed: Wikipedia 'Wardell Gray (February 13, 1921 – May 25, 1955) was an American jazz tenor saxophonist'. birthYear 1921, deathYear 1955, instruments [tenor saxophone] all match. Bio editorial (Bebop).

Entity confirmed: Wikipedia 'Booker Telleferro Ervin II (October 31, 1930 – August 31, 1970) was an American tenor saxophone player'. birthYear 1930, deathYear 1970, instruments [tenor saxophone] all match. Bio editorial (Hard Bop).

Entity confirmed: Wikipedia 'William McKinley "Red" Garland Jr. (May 13, 1923 – April 23, 1984) was an American modern jazz pianist'. birthYear 1923, deathYear 1984, instruments [piano] all match. Bio editorial (Bebop).

Opensearch resolved to a disambiguation stub, but websearch (resolved=true) confirms the correct page https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tommy_Flanagan_(musician): 'Thomas Lee Flanagan (March 16, 1930 – November 16, 2001) was an American jazz pianist'. birthYear 1930, deathYear 2001, instruments [piano] all match. Entity confirmed: Wikipedia 'Rahsaan Roland Kirk (born Ronald Theodore Kirk; August 7, 1935 – December 5, 1977)... played tenor saxophone, flute, and many other instruments'. birthYear 1935, deathYear 1977, instruments [tenor saxophone, flute] all match. Bio editorial (Hard Bop).

Entity confirmed: Wikipedia 'Oliver Edward Nelson (June 4, 1932 – October 28, 1975) was an American jazz saxophonist, clarinetist, arranger, composer, and bandleader'. birthYear 1932, deathYear 1975, instruments [saxophone, clarinet] all match. Bio editorial (Hard Bop).

Entity confirmed: Wikipedia 'Clifford Laconia Jordan (September 2, 1931 – March 27, 1993) was an American jazz tenor saxophone player and composer'. birthYear 1931, deathYear 1993, instruments [tenor saxophone] all match. Bio editorial (Hard Bop).

Entity confirmed: Wikipedia 'The Jazz Composer's Orchestra was an American jazz group, founded by Carla Bley and Michael Mantler in 1965'. Group entity, so birthYear is the founding year; deathYear null and instruments [] are correct placeholders for a group. NOTE for human reviewer: the lead says the orchestra was 'founded... in 1965', whereas our birthYear is 1966; the body also says 'A non-profit organisation was established in 1966, the Jazz Composers Orchestra Association Inc. (JCOA)'. The 1966 value maps to that JCOA association rather than the orchestra's 1965 founding. Not flagged as factual_error because the evidence supplies both years and the group's lineage (1964 October Revolution -> 1965 Jazz Composers Guild Orchestra -> renamed Jazz Composer's Orchestra) makes a single 'birth' year genuinely ambiguous; 1965 would be the more defensible value for the group itself.

Entity confirmed: Wikipedia 'Ernest Anthony Puente Jr. (April 20, 1923 – May 31, 2000)... bandleader, timbalero, vibraphonist'. birthYear 1923, deathYear 2000, instruments [vibraphone, timbales] all match. Bio framing 'mambo and Latin jazz' matches short description 'American Latin jazz and mambo musician'.

Entity confirmed: Wikipedia 'Woodrow Charles Herman (May 16, 1913 – October 29, 1987) was an American jazz clarinetist, saxophonist, singer, and big band leader'. birthYear 1913, deathYear 1987, instruments [saxophone, clarinet, vocals] all match (vocals = singer). Bio editorial (Swing).

No standalone Wikipedia article; websearch (resolved=true, source: Wikipedia 'Caribé') entity-matches the group. Every specific claim in the repaired prose bio is confirmed by the websearch note: 'led by conga player Juan Amalbert', 'Caribe... recorded with Eric Dolphy (recorded 1960, released New Jazz Feb 1961)', members 'Gene Casey (piano), Charlie Simons (vibraphone), Bill Ellington (bass), Manny Ramos (drums/timbales)', and 'Also recorded Latin Soul (1965)'. birthYear 0 / deathYear null / instruments [] are intentional group placeholders. Prior factually-wrong (Cool Jazz figure) bio has been correctly replaced.

Opensearch resolved to a disambiguation stub, but websearch (resolved=true) confirms https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clarence_Williams_(musician): 'Clarence Williams (October 8, 1898 or October 6, 1893 – November 6, 1965) was an American jazz pianist, composer...'. birthYear 1898 (first-listed of two candidate years; 1893 also appears in sources), deathYear 1965, instruments [piano] all match. Stored wikipediaUrl points to the (musician) page = correct (distinct from the actor Clarence Williams III).

Entity confirmed: Wikipedia 'Mamie Smith (née Robinson; May 26, 1891 – August or September 16, 1946) was an American singer... performed in multiple styles, including jazz and blues'. birthYear 1891, deathYear 1946, instruments [vocals] all match (singer). Era framing 'Early Jazz' is editorial; she made the first vocal blues recordings (1920) and is a foundational figure of the period. Not a flag.
